title: plantago coronopus subsp. occidentalis
intro: Plantago coronopus subsp. occidentalis, also known as Western Bugloss, is a subspecies of the Plantago coronopus plant, a type of flowering plant in the plantain family. Traditionally, the leaves of this plant have been used for their medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Western Bugloss include its ability to soothe digestive issues, reduce inflammation, and act as a natural diuretic. Western Bugloss can be prepared for herbal use through tea infusions, tinctures, or dried leaf infusions, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
